title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
hints: Reverse Polish Notation processes operands before the operator acts on them. What data structure naturally stores items to be used later in last-in-first-out order?
Use a stack to store operands. When you encounter an operator, pop the required number of values, apply the operation, and push the result back.
Be careful with division: it must truncate toward zero, not floor. Also, ensure you pop the second operand before the first to maintain correct order for non-commutative operations.
